name: New MCP server
description: Scaffold a Model Context Protocol server using the official SDK.
prompt:
I want to build a new MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. Help me scaffold it from scratch using the official @modelcontextprotocol/sdk:

1. Initialise a Node.js project in the current working directory (`npm init -y`) and add `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` as a dependency.
2. Create an `index.js` (or `index.ts`) that opens a stdio MCP server, registers at least one example tool (input schema + handler), and prints a friendly hello on startup so I know it's alive.
3. Add a short README.md that explains how I (or another AI) would: run the server locally; add it to my Claude / Synapse MCP client config; add a new tool.
4. Walk me through testing the example tool from the command line before we move on.

If I tell you what the server should DO, fold that into the scaffolding before generating the tool registry. Otherwise pick a small useful example (an `echo` tool) so the scaffold actually runs.

This project's environment has `SYNAPSE_FILES`, `SYNAPSE_SHARED_FILES`, `SYNAPSE_API`, `SYNAPSE_TOKEN`, and `SYNAPSE_PROJECT_ID` set -- you can `ls $SYNAPSE_FILES` to find any reference material I uploaded.
